items.title: Module loading baseline: reject the invalid model
items.prompt: Which statement correctly resolves this JavaScript: Language and Browser Applications misconception? Importing a value copies it permanently and each import re-executes the module.
hints: Compare both options with platform semantics and observable user or browser evidence.

feedback.correct: Correct. Module imports are live bindings and a module is evaluated once per realm after dependency linking.
feedback.incorrect: That preserves the misconception. Module imports are live bindings and a module is evaluated once per realm after dependency linking.

criteria.description: Selects the source-linked JavaScript: Language and Browser Applications correction rather than the documented misconception.

items.title: Module loading baseline: verified implementation facet
items.prompt: Load a module with deferred execution, strict semantics and explicit imports. Build a bounded artifact demonstrating strict module execution with lexical top-level semantics; the locked browser verifier checks observable structure, behavior, or computed presentation.

examEligibility.rationale: AI-assisted authoring draft. Runtime evidence does not replace independent human standards, pedagogy, accessibility, answer-oracle, and formal-exam review.

hints: Implement the smallest artifact that satisfies the declared observable contract, then check the visible case before considering the hidden boundary.

feedback.correct: The locked authoring verifier observed the declared bounded facet in both visible and hidden cases.
feedback.incorrect: At least one deterministic authoring check failed; inspect the exact artifact, interaction state, viewport, and observable result without assuming hidden-case details.

criteria.description: Passes the visible and hidden deterministic checks for the declared implementation facet in its pinned authoring environment.

items.starterCode: // TODO: implement the declared bounded browser behavior.

answer.referenceSolution: const value = 7; const doubled = value * 2; document.querySelector("output").textContent = String(doubled); globalThis.moduleThisIsUndefined = (function () { return this; })() === undefined;
answer.explanation: The reference is a bounded javascript artifact for strict module execution with lexical top-level semantics. Passing proves only the declared observable facet, not holistic mastery or human accessibility approval.
